1.o: All are the servants of God who are not slaves of sin and Satan. All gospel truth is according to godliness, teaching the fear of God. The intent of the gospel is to raise up hope as well as faith; to take off the mind and heart from the world, and to raise them to heaven and the things above. How excellent then is the gospel, which was the matter of Divine promise so early, and what thanks are due for our privileges! Faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God; and whoso is appointed and called, must preach the word. Grace is the free favour of God, and acceptance with him. Mercy, the fruits of the favour, in the pardon of sin, and freedom from all miseries both here and hereafter. And peace is the effect and fruit of mercy. Peace with God through Christ who is our Peace, and with the creatures and ourselves. Grace is the fountain of all blessings. Mercy, and peace, and all good, spring out of this.
1.m:
Everyone who isn’t controlled by sin and Satan serves God. Gospel truths teach us to respect God. The purpose of the gospel is to build faith and hope. It helps us focus on heaven instead of worldly things. The gospel has been promised since ancient times. We should be thankful for these gifts. 

Faith comes from listening to God’s word. People chosen to share the message must do so. Grace means God’s kindness toward us, accepting us even though we’re imperfect. Mercy includes forgiving sins and protecting us from troubles now and forever. Peace results from God’s mercy. This peace is found through Jesus, who brings harmony between people and with God. 

Grace is the source of all good things—mercy, peace, and more. These blessings flow from God’s favor.

1.o: The character and qualification of pastors, here called elders and bishops, agree with what the apostle wrote to Timothy. Being such bishops and overseers of the flock, to be examples to them, and God's stewards to take care of the affairs of his household, there is great reason that they should be blameless. What they are not to be, is plainly shown, as well as what they are to be, as servants of Christ, and able ministers of the letter and practice of the gospel. And here are described the spirit and practice becoming such as should be examples of good works.
1.m:
Pastors, who are also called elders and bishops, match what the apostle told Timothy about them. As leaders of the church, they need to set an example for others and manage God’s work carefully. They should avoid doing anything wrong since they are responsible for taking care of God’s family. 

They shouldn’t do certain things, just like they should do other things, if they want to serve Christ well and teach people about God’s message. The qualities and actions of these leaders should show everyone what good behavior looks like.

1.o: False teachers are described. Faithful ministers must oppose such in good time, that their folly being made manifest, they may go no further They had a base end in what they did; serving a worldly interest under pretence of religion: for the love of money is the root of all evil. Such should be resisted, and put to shame, by sound doctrine from the Scriptures. Shameful actions, the reproach of heathens, should be far from Christians; falsehood and lying, envious craft and cruelty, brutal and sensual practices, and idleness and sloth, are sins condemned even by the light of nature. But Christian meekness is as far from cowardly passing over sin and error, as from anger and impatience. And though there may be national differences of character, yet the heart of man in every age and place is deceitful and desperately wicked. But the sharpest reproofs must aim at the good of the reproved; and soundness in the faith is most desirable and necessary. To those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure; they abuse, and turn things lawful and good into sin. Many profess to know God, yet in their lives deny and reject him. See the miserable state of hypocrites, such as have a form of godliness, but are without the power; yet let us not be so ready to fix this charge on others, as careful that it does not apply to ourselves.
1.m: False teachers need to be stopped early so their mistakes become obvious and they don’t cause more trouble. Their goal was selfish, using religion to get ahead in the world. Loving money causes lots of problems. We must fight against them with true teachings from the Bible. Christians shouldn't do shameful things that bring disgrace, like lying, jealousy, hurting others, or being lazy. Meekness means standing up to wrong behavior without getting angry. People everywhere are sinful, no matter where they live. When we correct someone, our goal should be to help them improve. True beliefs are very important. Those who act badly see everything as bad too. Some people pretend to follow God but actually ignore him through their actions. Hypocrites look religious but lack real goodness. Be careful not to call others hypocrites while making sure you aren’t one yourself.

2.o: Old disciples of Christ must behave in every thing agreeably to the Christian doctrine. That the aged men be sober; not thinking that the decays of nature will justify any excess; but seeking comfort from nearer communion with God, not from any undue indulgence. Faith works by, and must be seen in love, of God for himself, and of men for God's sake. Aged persons are apt to be peevish and fretful; therefore need to be on their guard. Though there is not express Scripture for every word, or look, yet there are general rules, according to which all must be ordered. Young women must be sober and discreet; for many expose themselves to fatal temptations by what at first might be only want of discretion. The reason is added, that the word of God may not be blasphemed. Failures in duties greatly reproach Christianity. Young men are apt to be eager and thoughtless, therefore must be earnestly called upon to be sober-minded: there are more young people ruined by pride than by any other sin. Every godly man's endeavour must be to stop the mouths of adversaries. Let thine own conscience answer for thine uprightness. What a glory is it for a Christian, when that mouth which would fain open itself against him, cannot find any evil in him to speak of!
2.m:
Old followers of Christ should act in ways that match Christian teachings. Older men should stay sober. They shouldn't think that getting older gives them an excuse to do wrong things. Instead, they should find comfort in being closer to God, not through bad behavior.

Faith shows itself in loving God and others because of God. Older people sometimes get grumpy and upset easily. So they need to watch out for that.

Even if the Bible doesn’t mention every little detail about behavior, we still have overall rules to follow. 

Young women should be careful too. Many get into trouble because they aren’t thoughtful enough. This way, people won’t insult the words of God. When Christians fail in their duties, it hurts the reputation of Christianity.

Young men often rush into things without thinking. They need to be careful and keep a clear mind. Pride ruins more young lives than any other sin. Each good person tries to silence those who speak badly about them.

It’s wonderful when someone who wants to criticize you can’t find anything wrong to say.

2.o: Servants must know and do their duty to their earthly masters, with a reference to their heavenly one. In serving an earthly master according to Christ's will, He is served; such shall be rewarded by him. Not giving disrespectful or provoking language; but to take a check or reproof with silence, not making confident or bold replies. When conscious of a fault, to excuse or justify it, doubles it. Never putting to their own use that which is their master's, nor wasting the goods they are trusted with. Showing all good fidelity to improve a master's goods, and promote his thriving. If ye have not been faithful in that which is another man's, who shall give you that which is your own? Lu 16:12. True religion is an honour to the professors of it; and they should adorn it in all things.
2.m:
Servants need to know and follow their duties to their human bosses while thinking about God too. Serving an earthly boss well means serving Jesus. This kind of service earns rewards from Him. 

Don’t talk back or say mean things. Instead, accept corrections quietly without arguing. Don’t try to blame others for mistakes or explain away faults. That just makes them worse.

Never steal from your boss or waste what belongs to them. Be honest and work hard to help their business grow. 

If you’re not trustworthy with someone else’s stuff, how can you expect to handle your own properly? Luke 16:12. People who truly believe in God should show it through everything they do. They should live in ways that bring honor to their faith.

2.o: The doctrine of grace and salvation by the gospel, is for all ranks and conditions of men. It teaches to forsake sin; to have no more to do with it. An earthly, sensual conversation suits not a heavenly calling. It teaches to make conscience of that which is good. We must look to God in Christ, as the object of our hope and worship. A gospel conversation must be a godly conversation. See our duty in a very few words; denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, living soberly, righteously, and godly, notwithstanding all snares, temptations, corrupt examples, ill usage, and what remains of sin in the believer's heart, with all their hinderances. It teaches to look for the glories of another world. At, and in, the glorious appearing of Christ, the blessed hope of Christians will be complete: To bring us to holiness and happiness was the end of Christ's death. Jesus Christ, that great God and our Saviour, who saves not only as God, much less as Man alone; but as God-man, two natures in one person. He loved us, and gave himself for us; and what can we do less than love and give up ourselves to him! Redemption from sin and sanctification of the nature go together, and make a peculiar people unto God, free from guilt and condemnation, and purified by the Holy Spirit. All Scripture is profitable. Here is what will furnish for all parts of duty, and the right discharge of them. Let us inquire whether our whole dependence is placed upon that grace which saves the lost, pardons the guilty, and sanctifies the unclean. And the further we are removed from boasting of fancied good works, or trusting in them, so that we glory in Christ alone, the more zealous shall we be to abound in real good works.
2.m:
The message about grace and salvation through the gospel applies to everyone. It tells us to stop sinning and leave it behind. Living an earthly life focused on pleasures doesn’t fit someone called to heaven. 

It shows us to care about doing what’s right. We should trust in God in Christ as our hope and focus of worship. Following the gospel means living a godly life. In just a few words, it tells us to reject evil behavior and live sensibly, fairly, and faithfully despite challenges, bad influences, mistreatment, and even the sins still within us.

It also reminds us to look forward to the wonderful things waiting in the next world. When Jesus returns in glory, he will fulfill the hopes of his followers. His purpose in dying was to make us holy and happy. As both God and man, Jesus loves us and gave himself for us. What else can we do except love and give ourselves to him?

Being saved from sin and having our hearts made pure happen together. This makes us God’s special people, free from guilt and cleansed by the Holy Spirit. The Bible helps us with everything we need to do our duties well. We should check if we rely fully on God’s grace that saves us, forgives us, and purifies us. The less we depend on our supposed good deeds or try to earn favor, and the more we trust only in Christ, the more eager we’ll be to truly serve him.

3.o: Spiritual privileges do not make void or weaken, but confirm civil duties. Mere good words and good meanings are not enough without good works. They were not to be quarrelsome, but to show meekness on all occasions, not toward friends only, but to all men, though with wisdom, Jas 3:13. And let this text teach us how wrong it is for a Christian to be churlish to the worst, weakest, and most abject. The servants of sin have many masters, their lusts hurry them different ways; pride commands one thing, covetousness another. Thus they are hateful, deserving to be hated. It is the misery of sinners, that they hate one another; and it is the duty and happiness of saints to love one another. And we are delivered out of our miserable condition, only by the mercy and free grace of God, the merit and sufferings of Christ, and the working of his Spirit. God the Father is God our Saviour. He is the fountain from which the Holy Spirit flows, to teach, regenerate, and save his fallen creatures; and this blessing comes to mankind through Christ. The spring and rise of it, is the kindness and love of God to man. Love and grace have, through the Spirit, great power to change and turn the heart to God. Works must be in the saved, but are not among the causes of their salvation. A new principle of grace and holiness is wrought, which sways, and governs, and makes the man a new creature. Most pretend they would have heaven at last, yet they care not for holiness now; they would have the end without the beginning. Here is the outward sign and seal thereof in baptism, called therefore the washing of regeneration. The work is inward and spiritual; this is outwardly signified and sealed in this ordinance. Slight not this outward sign and seal; yet rest not in the outward washing, but look to the answer of a good conscience, without which the outward washing will avail nothing. The worker therein is the Spirit of God; it is the renewing of the Holy Ghost. Through him we mortify sin, perform duty, walk in God's ways; all the working of the Divine life in us, and the fruits of righteousness without, are through this blessed and holy Spirit. The Spirit and his saving gifts and graces, come through Christ, as a Saviour, whose undertaking and work are to bring to grace and glory. Justification, in the gospel sense, is the free forgiveness of a sinner; accepting him as righteous through the righteousness of Christ received by faith. God, in justifying a sinner in the way of the gospel, is gracious to him, yet just to himself and his law. As forgiveness is through a perfect righteousness, and satisfaction is made to justice by Christ, it cannot be merited by the sinner himself. Eternal life is set before us in the promise; the Spirit works faith in us, and hope of that life; faith and hope bring it near, and fill with joy in expectation of it.
3.m:
Spiritual blessings don’t excuse people from doing their civic duties. Good intentions aren’t enough—people need to act on them too. Christians shouldn't argue but should always be gentle, even with those who are difficult. James 3:13 tells us to be wise and humble. This means being kind to everyone, including those who might not deserve it. People controlled by sin often follow many masters, like pride and greed, which pull them in different directions. Sinners tend to dislike each other, while believers should love one another. We are saved only by God’s mercy, Jesus’ sacrifice, and the work of His Spirit. God the Father is our Savior. He sends the Holy Spirit to teach, transform, and save us through Jesus. Grace and love come from God’s kindness towards us. These things help change hearts and make people new. Being saved includes having good actions, but these actions don’t cause salvation. Instead, a new spirit of goodness is planted inside us, guiding us to become better people. Many claim they want heaven someday but don’t care about living right now. They want the reward without putting in the effort. Baptism is a symbol of this inner change, called the washing of regeneration. The real transformation happens inside, but baptism shows it publicly. Don’t ignore baptism, but remember it doesn’t replace a clean conscience. The Holy Spirit does the work of changing us and helping us live right. Through Him, we overcome sin, do what is right, and follow God’s path. All the good things we do and the changes in our lives happen because of the Holy Spirit.

The Holy Spirit and all the good gifts he gives come through Jesus, who saves us and brings us to grace and glory. In the gospel, being justified means getting forgiven freely because we trust in Jesus' righteousness. When God forgives sinners through the gospel, he shows mercy while still being fair to his laws. Since forgiveness comes from Jesus’ perfect righteousness and he paid for our sins, we can’t earn it ourselves. The promise of eternal life is given to us. The Holy Spirit helps us believe in that promise and hope for that life. Faith and hope bring us closer to that life and fill us with joy about it.

3.o: When the grace of God towards mankind has been declared, the necessity of good works is pressed. Those who believe in God, must make it their care to maintain good works, to seek opportunities for doing them, being influenced by love and gratitude. Trifling, foolish questions must be avoided, and subtle distinctions and vain inquiries; nor should people be eager after novelties, but love sound doctrine which tends most to edifying. Though we may now think some sins light and little, if the Lord awaken the conscience, we shall feel even the smallest sin heavy upon our souls.
3.m: When God’s kindness toward us has been explained, the importance of doing good deeds is emphasized. People who believe in God need to focus on performing good actions out of love and thankfulness. They should avoid silly debates and pointless questions. Instead, they should value teachings that build up faith. Even small sins can weigh heavily on us if God makes us aware of them.

3.o: Christianity is not a fruitless profession; and its professors must be filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are by Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God. They must be doing good, as well as keeping away from evil. Let “ours” follow some honest labour and employment, to provide for themselves and their families. Christianity obliges all to seek some honest work and calling, and therein to abide with God. The apostle concludes with expressions of kind regard and fervent prayer. Grace be with you all; the love and favour of God, with the fruits and effects thereof, according to need; and the increase and feeling of them more and more in your souls. This is the apostle's wish and prayer, showing his affection to them, and desire for their good, and would be a means of obtaining for them, and bringing down on them, the thing requested. Grace is the chief thing to be wished and prayed for, with respect to ourselves or others; it is “all good.”
3.m:
Being a Christian isn't pointless. Followers of Christ should do good things and stay away from bad ones. We should have jobs that are honest and help us take care of our families. 

Christianity teaches everyone to find an honest way to earn money and stick with it. 

The writer ends by saying he cares about these people and prays for them. He wishes they will receive God’s love and favor, along with the good results of that love. He hopes they feel those blessings more and more deeply inside.

This shows how much the writer loves them and wants what’s best for them. His prayers aim to bring God’s grace to them. Grace includes everything good we could ask for.
